title: MD
biography: Edward Jenner was an English physician who pioneered the concept of vaccination by demonstrating that cowpox inoculation protected against smallpox in 1796. Often called the 'Father of Immunology,' his work is estimated to have saved more lives than any other person in history.
aiSummary: Edward Jenner was the Father of Immunology who invented vaccination using cowpox to prevent smallpox in 1796. His work has saved more lives than any other medical innovation. Based in Berkeley, United Kingdom.

timeline.title: First vaccination experiment
timeline.description: Inoculated James Phipps with cowpox material from dairymaid Sarah Nelmes, then showed he was immune to smallpox

timeline.title: Published 'An Inquiry into the Causes and Effects of the Variolae Vaccinae'
timeline.description: Published the landmark paper that introduced vaccination to the world

timeline.title: Received parliamentary grant
timeline.description: British Parliament granted £10,000 in recognition of his discovery

timeline.title: Smallpox eradicated globally
timeline.description: WHO declared smallpox eradicated, the culmination of Jenner's 1796 discovery
